success_surface: Multi-sheet synchronization using reference data with hybrid pandas-openpyxl approach
effective_behaviors: Combined pandas for data transformation with openpyxl for workbook-level preservation
Extracted reference sheet into dictionary for efficient cross-sheet lookups
Encapsulated repetitive sheet update logic into reusable functions
Cleared target sheet regions before writing to prevent stale data conflicts
Explicitly wrote header rows to maintain table structure integrity
Normalized data types during key matching to ensure reliable comparisons
Mapped pandas DataFrame indices to Excel row numbers accounting for 1-based indexing
evidence: Used load_workbook() alongside pd.read_excel() to leverage both tools' strengths
Created items_dict = dict(zip(items_df['S.N'].astype(str), items_df['ITEM'])) for O(1) lookups
Defined update_sheet_with_items() function applied to both SHEET1 and SHEET2
Looped through ws_sheet1.iter_rows() setting c.value = None before writing new data
Set ws_sheet1.cell(row=1, column=1).value = 'S.N' and column=2 for ITEM headers
Applied str() conversion when comparing S.N values: sn = str(row['S.N'])
Calculated row_num = idx + 2 to account for header row at position 1

memory_items.title: Hybrid Spreadsheet Engine Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Use pandas for bulk data manipulation and openpyxl for direct cell access
memory_items.generalization: Load reference data with pandas for efficiency, then use openpyxl Workbook objects to write final output, combining the best of both libraries without format loss.

memory_items.title: Cross-Sheet Reference Dictionary
memory_items.lesson: Convert a reference sheet to a dictionary keyed by identifier columns
memory_items.generalization: Build a lookup dictionary from a master/reference sheet using relevant key-value pairs to enable fast value matching across multiple worksheets.

memory_items.title: Reusable Sheet Update Function
memory_items.lesson: Wrap repetitive multi-sheet logic in functions for consistency
memory_items.generalization: When modifying multiple sheets with identical or similar operations, encapsulate the logic in a function to reduce errors and ensure uniform application across all targets.

memory_items.title: Target Range Clearance Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Clear existing cell values before writing new data to affected ranges
memory_items.generalization: Iterate through the target worksheet region and nullify cell values before populating with updated data to eliminate residual content from previous executions.

memory_items.title: Index-to-Row Alignment Handling
memory_items.lesson: Account for header row offset when mapping DataFrame indices to Excel rows
memory_items.generalization: Add appropriate offset to pandas DataFrame indices (typically +1 or +2) to align with 1-based Excel row numbering, especially when headers occupy the first row(s).

edits.content:
## Cross-Sheet Synchronization Patterns

### Hybrid Tool Integration
When tasks require both data computation and file preservation:
- Process transformations with pandas for performance and clarity
- Use openpyxl Workbook objects to write final results, avoiding `to_excel` formatting loss
- Load reference data once, convert to lookup structures, apply across sheets

### Multi-Sheet Update Consistency
For synchronized updates across multiple worksheets:
- Encapsulate shared logic in reusable functions parameterized by sheet name
- Build dictionary lookups from authoritative/reference sheets using key columns
- Apply identical validation rules to all target sheets uniformly

### Safe In-Place Replacement Workflow
When overwriting worksheet content:
- Clear the target range iteratively before writing new values
- Write headers explicitly before data rows to preserve structure
- Account for Excel's 1-based row indexing when iterating from pandas (usually row_num = df_index + 2)
- Normalize data types (strings for keys, etc.) during comparison operations

### Post-Operation Verification
Always include sanity checks after modifications:
- Verify row counts match expected ranges
- Spot-check sample cells for correct value replacement
- Print summary statistics comparing original vs. modified state
